v. n. 1. To slink, slip, or run away; sheer off; disappear. 1. Āyā jo chugal-khor, to bandah vahīṅ saṭkā, Woh ṭakkareṅ khātā huā phirtārahā bhaṭaktā . Nazīr. My tell-tale rival come, your slave just slipped away, And left him wandering, groping, stumbling, all astray. 2. To swallow a pill. 3. (E. suṛuknā ) to gulp down. Sāṅcho rasyā jhūṭ na bole, toë pānī ke sāth saṭak jāūṅgo, Gorī! banjā mirach! kuṭak jāuṅgo! Rus. song. A lover true speaks not untrue, with water I'll just gulp you down, O fair one! be a chilly do! crunched 'tween my teeth't will just go down.

ety: satā˚ = Prk. संताव(इ), or संतावे(इ) = S. सन्तापय(ति), caus. of सम्+तप्
def: v.t. To pain, torment, torture, distress, harass, persecute, oppress, inflict injury upon, afflict, harm; to trouble, grieve, tease, annoy, vex, worry, molest, interrupt
more: v. a. S. सन्तापन executing. 1. To molest; harass; trouble; annoy. 2. To persecute; oppress; afflict; give pain to. 1. Birah ne satāī, maiṅ jāg uṭhī; jal jāë woh sej kasī kī kasī! Wom. song. The pangs of separation felt I start and wake in bed! Fire burn a bed tight strung and smooth as when't was just new made! 2. Bhūt jān na māre, satā māre . Prov. The evil spirit kills not but afflicts. 3. Jo chāhe tauṅ āpna din aur rain bhalā Phakkar aur ādhīn ko mūlan nāṅh satā . Prov. O man! if peace thou wouldst have night and day From hurt of poor and beggar thine hand stay! 3. To exact; take by force. Chaprāsī be-satāë nahīṅ rahte . Prov. saying. satāne-vālā, satāū , adj. 1. Pernicious; noxious; injurious; hurtful; offensive. 2. Harassing ( dukhdāī ). satāne-vālā, satāū , n. m. Persecutor; oppressor.
v. a. To tease, vex, fret, to trouble, afflict, ail, harm, interrupt, annoy, grieve, harass.

adv. S. सहित With; together; along with; inclusive; concurrent; concomitant. 1. Sāth soī, bāt khoī . Prov. Once slept together the new zest gone for ever. (See bāt , 59.) 2. Dīyā hāth, khāne lagā sāth . Prov. Your hand to him extend, at your dinner see your friend. 3. Halvāī kī jāī, aur sove sāth qasāī . Prov. A confectioner's daughter, and gone with a butcher!
more: n. m. Society; company. 1. Paisā gāṅṭh kā, jorū sāth kī . Prov. Your pence about your person hide, your wife have ever by your side. 2. Sāth jorū khasam kā . Prov. The only true partner ship is between man and wife. 3. Sāth ke līye bhāt chhoṛā jātā hai . Prov. For love and company he left his rice-meal free. 4. Paidal aur savār kā kyā sāth ? Prov. Footmen and riders are not company. (Drunk and sober cant agree). 5. Lauṅḍī kī zāt kyā, raṅḍī kā sāth kyā. Bheṛ kī lāt kyā, aurat kī bāt kyā ? Prov. What is a slave girl's dignity, what boots a harlot's company? Who minds a sheep's kick, who on words of woman weak could e'er rely? 6. Jab-tak rakābī meṅ bhāt, merā terā sāth . Prov. While rice is in your dish I'm with you as you wish. sāth iske . 1. Along with this. 2. Besides; moreover. sāth uske . 1. Along with that. 2. Although; notwithstanding. sāth-uṅchāī yā akās-choṭī ḍhalāo . ( Ast .) Zeni…

def: s.m. A kind of mystical mark made on persons or things to denote good luck (it is shaped like a Greek cross with the extremities of the four arms bent round in the same direction: Hindūs prefix the mark in vermilion at the beginning of their account-books at the commencement of a new year; and the same mark is made on the ground with flour at marriages and other ceremonies).

def: v. n. To be superannuated, decrepit, or in one's dotage. 1. Beṭā barje bāp ko, 'tu hai gayo saṭhiyāë, Nāj bigāṛe ghar rahe, sab se dūnā khāë! ' Dohā. The son the father rates, 'you are just in your dotage now, You stick at home and waste the food, eat twice your share, I vow.' 2. Kuchh khair hai, baṛe miyāṅ! saṭhiyā gaë ho kyā? Laṛkoṅ ke sāth khelne tum geṛiyāṅ chale! Rāhat. Are you in your right mind, old man, or is your dotage come? To play with children bat and ball does n't you become.

Its shape is a Greek cross, with the extremities of the four arms bent round in the same direction, It is made with vermilion by Hindus at the beginning of their account-books at the commencement of a new year. It is a symbol of the goddess Kāli and it signifies the obligation to make true and faithful entries. The same figure is formed on the ground with flour at marriages and other ceremonies. Merī naṅṛdal jī ko tum na bulāo, mere saiyāṅ! Māṅgegī divlā balāī, more saiyāṅ! māngegī sathye dharāī, morī saiyāṅ! Wom. song. Your sister dont invite, my husband, pray! def: v.t. To press together; to rub together; to rub, smear, daub; to rub cloth with fuller's earth or with mud (preparatory to washing); to mash, to mix, mingle; to knead; to wash out, rinse (cf. sānnā).
more: v. a. S. शोधन 1. To mix. Masālah sauṅd sāṅdh-ke gosht chaṛhā do! Mix the condiments well with the meat and set the pot on the fire! 2. To smear; rub over with fuller's earth.
v. a. 1. To rub cloth in mud preparatory to washing. 2. To mix, to smear.

def: s.m. (f: -ī), A distiller and vendor of spirituous liquors, a vintner, tavern-keeper.

v.t. = sauṅdhnā, q.v. v.t. To press together; to rub together; to rub, smear, daub; to rub cloth with fuller's earth or with mud (preparatory to washing); to mash, to mix, mingle; to knead; to wash out, rinse (cf. sānnā).
s.f. Anise seed, Pimpinella anisum; the seed of sweet fennel, Anethum fœniculum, or A. panmorium (cf. soʼā and soyā)
more: n. f. Anise seed; Pimpinella anisum ; the seed of sweet fennel; Anethum fœniculum panmorium , Wat. sauṅf kā arq, arq-i-bādyān , n. m. ( Med .) A nise water.
subs: s.m. Extract of anise seed, anise-water; dill-water.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = swāṅg, q.v. s.m. Imitation, mimicry, &c. ( = sāṅg, q.v.). s.f. Spear, javelin, dart (all of iron), a goad; — a borer for wells.
s.f. = sāṅgī, q.v. s.m. Mimic, actor, player.
s. f. See سانگي sāngī.
adv. S. स्म्मुख See sāmne .
s.m. A vendor of the flesh of beasts or birds; butcher; poulterer; — hunting, chase.
s.m. (f. -ī), A vendor of the flesh of beasts or birds; a butcher; a poulterer; — hunting, the chase

ety: saun, 'omen,' q.v.+iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. An augur; a soothsayer.
s.f. Charge, custody, protection, care.
s.f. = sauṅf, q.v. s.f. Anise seed, Pimpinella anisum; the seed of sweet fennel, Anethum fœniculum, or A. panmorium (cf. soʼā and soyā)
subs: s.m. Extract of anise seed, anise-water; dill-water.

ety: sauṅp˚ = Prk. समप्प(इ), or समप्पे(इ) = S. समर्पय(ति), caus. of rt. सम्+ऋ
def: v.t. To deliver over, to hand or make over, to consign, give, intrust (to), deposit (with); to give in charge, to commit; to give up, resign, surrender, cede
more: v. a. S. समर्पण To deliver over; vest in; invest; cede. Kahā Haq ko sauṅpā, tujhe le sidhār! Mīr Hasan.
v. a. To deliver over, commit to charge, give, consign, resign, intrust, deposite.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = soʼar, q.v
s.m. Anything black; black; soot; lamp-black, &c
more: s. m. Soot, or any thing of a black colour.

def: s.m. (dialec.) The world; affairs of the world; — family, wife and children; progeny; household affairs, housewifery.
more: s. (dakh.) Family, progeny: see قبايل ḳabāʼịl.

def: s.f. (dialec.) Housewife; dame.
adj. Connected with or relating to sleep, somnolent; — s.m. Nocturnal combat; a night-attack.

s.m. A bull; the constellation Taurus, one of the signs of the Zodiac; the redness rising and spreading in the faint light that is seen above the horizon between sunset and nightfall; twilight, crepuscule; — the name of a mountain near Mecca.
more: (Heb. ) s. m. 1. A bull. 2. Taurus, the sign of the Zodiack. 3. Twilight, crepuscule. 4. The name of a mountain near Mecca.

ety: prob. S. शकुलः; cf. S. शष्कुली, and H. saurī
def: s.m. A kind of fish.
more: n. f. H. so sleep, oṛh cover. A quilt. Rāt sauṛ māṅ jāḍā lāgā jib ūpar kāṅbal ḍātā, jāḍe ne āj gajab ghālā . W. Rus. song.
1. ( सौर ) adj. Solar. 2. ( शकुल ) Name of a fish.

def: adj. Belonging or relating to the sun (sūra), solar; sacred to the sun; — s.m. A solar month; a solar day; a worshipper of the sun; — the planet Saturn
s.m. Fragrance, odour; pleasingness, agreeableness; beauty; good reputation, fame; (S. saurabham) saffron; — the mango-tree.
v. n. (dakh. i. q. بي تاب هونا) To be unable or impatient (?).
s.m. Heroism, &c. ( = sūrtā, q.v.). adj. (f. -ī), Considerate, thoughtful, mindful, attentive, careful; accurate; intelligent, sensible, prudent.
v.n. (Dakkhinī), To become faint or weary; to be or become helpless; to lose patience, or the power of endurance
more: v. n. (dakh. i. q. بي تاب هونا) To be unable or impatient (?).

def: s.f. = saurāth, q.v. s.f. (prov. or dialec.) Selfishness, greed, avarice; excessive desire, eagerness; ambition.
more: s. f. A festival held on the fourteenth of the dark fortnight in the month of Phālguna (February-March) in honour of the birth of the linga or phallus.
s. f. (dakh. i. q. حرص) Avarice, greediness, eager desire, ambition.

ety: for A. سورة, v.n. fr. سور 'to assault,' &c.
def: s.f. Force, strength; overpowering influence; fierceness, impetuousness, impetus, violence; outburst; vehemence; tyranny, despotism.

ety: prob. corr. of S. स्वार्थ, q.v.
def: s.f. (prov. or dialec.) Selfishness, greed, avarice; excessive desire, eagerness; ambition.
